title: cxxrtl should not be counting steps in `module::step()`
body:
This was introduced in commit https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/commit/c382d7d3ac2b713c5b3f55e81430e121520a1787.

There are several issues with this approach:
- There's no guarantee `module::step()` gets called at all. For example it is completely OK to call `cxxrtl_eval` and `cxxrtl_commit` directly, and Amaranth does so.
- Even if it's called, it will not be called in submodules (since for those, `eval` and `commit` get called directly), so time will always be 0 inside of those.
- In general CXXRTL has no notion of time (at the time being anyway), so the formatter should probably print `$time`/`$realtime` (what is `$realtime` anyway?) as it is (expecting something on the stdout to filter the output) or use some sort of escaping mechanism.
